nodes.title: Support both string paths and segment arrays
nodes.why: In real projects, path can come from different places. This accepts both formats without forcing callers to convert first.
nodes.tradeoff: A tiny bit more branching, but the API is easier to use.

nodes.title: Treat empty path as the root value
nodes.why: An empty path should mean “don’t go deeper”. Returning no segments lets the function return the original object.
nodes.alternative: Throw an error for empty path.
nodes.tradeoff: Softer behavior, but it requires one extra rule to remember.

nodes.title: Fallback immediately when path format is invalid
nodes.why: If path is neither string nor array, we stop early instead of trying to traverse with bad input.
nodes.alternative: Throw a TypeError for invalid path types.
nodes.tradeoff: More forgiving API, but less strict signaling to callers.

nodes.title: Stop traversal on null/undefined intermediates
nodes.why: This prevents runtime crashes when a middle level is missing.
nodes.alternative: Access cur[key] directly and rely on try/catch.
nodes.tradeoff: Extra guard check per step, but safer and clearer flow.

nodes.title: Check key existence, not value truthiness
nodes.why: This keeps valid values like 0, false, and "" from being treated as missing.
nodes.tradeoff: Slightly less compact, but much more correct for edge cases.

nodes.title: Return the resolved value exactly as found
nodes.why: If the path exists, we return whatever is there, including false, null, or undefined.
nodes.alternative: Return cur || fallback at the end.
nodes.tradeoff: You must rely on the existence checks earlier, but output stays accurate.
